# T3 Golden Fixture

This directory contains a small set of **real T3 artifacts** captured from
T3's own published test data. They are checked in so that Carmel's
parser/normalization tests are deterministic and do not require running
T3 (which currently cannot import on Python 3.12 — see
`docs/development.md`).

## Provenance

Files under `sample_project/` are copied verbatim from
[`ReactionMechanismGenerator/T3`](https://github.com/ReactionMechanismGenerator/T3)
under `tests/data/`:

| Carmel path | T3 source path |
|---|---|
| `sample_project/input.yml` | `tests/data/functional_2_thermo/input.yml` |
| `sample_project/iteration_1/ARC/T3_info.yml` | `tests/data/restart/r6/iteration_6/ARC/T3_info.yml` |
| `sample_project/iteration_2/ARC/T3_info.yml` | `tests/data/process_arc/iteration_2/ARC/T3_info.yml` |
| `sample_project/iteration_1/RMG/pdep/network1_1.py` | `tests/data/pdep_network/iteration_1/RMG/pdep/network1_1.py` |
| `sample_project/iteration_1/RMG/pdep/network4_1.py` | `tests/data/pdep_network/iteration_1/RMG/pdep/network4_1.py` |
| `sample_project/t3.log` | placeholder marker (real `t3.log` files are large) |

These were originally generated by running T3 on real chemistry inputs
and committed to the T3 repo as test data. We use them as a captured
"oracle" for Carmel's `normalize_t3_outputs()` so we can validate
behavior without running T3.

## What the fixture proves

The `TestGoldenFixture` class in `tests/test_t3_adapter.py` loads this
fixture and asserts:

- `T3_info.yml` parsing yields the right `species` labels with their
  `success` status
- iterating across multiple `iteration_*` directories aggregates species
  across iterations
- the `qm.level_of_theory` field is correctly extracted from the input
  YAML (since T3 never writes LOT into output)
- PDep network files in `RMG/pdep/network*.py` are correctly counted
  and their stems used as network IDs

## Re-capturing

If T3's output schema changes, re-capture by:

1. Running a tiny real T3 job (the T3 `examples/minimal/` input is the
   smallest)
2. Copying the resulting `iteration_*/ARC/T3_info.yml`, the input file,
   and a couple of `iteration_*/RMG/pdep/network*.py` files into
   `sample_project/`
3. Updating the assertions in `TestGoldenFixture` to match the new
   ground truth
